content: I just got back from a 6-month sabbatical traveling through Eastern Europe, and I'm feeling inspired to learn some new skills. My 46th birthday is coming up and I want to make it meaningful.
Welcome back! Eastern Europe must have been incredible. What countries did you visit?

I started in Prague, then went through Budapest, Bucharest, Sofia, and ended in Istanbul. I was particularly fascinated by the architecture and design aesthetics in each city. As an interior designer for commercial spaces, it really opened my eyes.
That sounds amazing! The architecture in those cities is stunning. Did anything specific inspire you for your work?

Definitely. I loved the Art Nouveau details in Budapest and the minimalist Soviet-era buildings in Bucharest. I'm thinking of incorporating some of those elements into my next hotel lobby design project.
Those would make for such unique designs! What new skills are you considering learning?

I've been wanting to get better at woodworking - I have a small workshop at home and I'd love to make some custom furniture pieces. I also want to improve my Thai cooking skills. I took a cooking class in Bangkok during my trip and was blown away.
Woodworking and Thai cooking are quite different but both very rewarding! Do you have any specific projects in mind for woodworking?

I'm thinking of building a custom dining table for my home. I've been brewing my own beer for about 3 years now, so I'd love to make a table with built-in beer taps. My chess club meets at my house every Thursday too, so it needs to be big enough for games.
A dining table with beer taps sounds incredible! That would be perfect for your chess nights. Have you worked with wood on that scale before?

I've made smaller pieces like cutting boards and a coffee table, but nothing this ambitious. I'm also thinking of learning calligraphy to create custom labels for my home-brewed beers. I've been documenting my travels on my blog too, so maybe I could incorporate that somehow.
That's a great combination of skills! Calligraphy would make your beer labels look professional. How do you plan to learn woodworking and calligraphy?

There's a woodworking class starting next month at the community college. For calligraphy, I found an online course by a famous calligrapher I follow on Instagram. I'm planning to start both in January.
That's a solid plan! The community college class will give you hands-on guidance for the table project. I'm excited to see how you combine all these new skills together!
